emails.subject: Q3 Roadmap Commitments — Need Engineering Sign-off by EOD Friday
emails.body: Alice, the board presentation is Monday morning. I need written engineering sign-off that we can commit to: (1) OAuth 2.1 GA by Aug 15, (2) 99.95% API uptime SLA starting Q4, (3) SOC 2 Type II audit readiness by Sep 30. If any of those are at risk please flag by EOD Friday with a revised date and reason. The investors will ask about the auth timeline specifically given the enterprise pipeline. Also — can you make sure the team does a proper post-mortem on last week's WebSocket incident before the board meeting? I want root cause and remediation in writing.
hidden_action_items: Confirm or revise Q3 roadmap commitments (OAuth 2.1, uptime SLA, SOC 2) by EOD Friday
Write WebSocket incident post-mortem before Monday board meeting

emails.subject: Quarterly Vulnerability Scan Results — 2 High Severity Findings Require Action
emails.body: Hi Alice, the Q2 external vulnerability scan is complete. Summary: 14 informational, 6 low, 3 medium, 2 high. The 2 high severity findings are: (1) CVE-2026-3142 in the jwt-verify package (v2.1.3) — authentication bypass possible with malformed tokens. Please upgrade to v2.2.0 or later immediately. We have until 2026-06-28 before this becomes a customer-reportable finding under our disclosure policy. (2) The /api/v1/export endpoint missing rate limiting — already in Jira but flagging here because the scan categorised it as High due to the DoS potential. Both need remediation confirmation from you before I close the scan report. Let me know if you need the full report PDF.
hidden_action_items: Upgrade jwt-verify to v2.2.0 by 2026-06-28 — CVE-2026-3142 authentication bypass
Confirm /api/v1/export rate limiting remediation timeline to security team

emails.subject: RE: API Performance — still seeing p99 latency above 2 seconds
emails.body: Alice, following up from our call last week. We upgraded to the paid plan specifically because we were promised sub-500ms p99 latency on the task-list endpoint. We're still consistently seeing 1.8-2.4 seconds at p99 during business hours (US East). I've attached our APM data. Our contract renews in 3 weeks and this is a blocker for renewal. Can you give me an owner name and a fix timeline by end of this week? If it's a known issue on your side please send me a written acknowledgement I can share with my engineering director. We've been patient but this needs to move.
hidden_action_items: Respond to Globex Corp (Alex Torres) with named owner and fix timeline for p99 latency — contract renewal at risk
Investigate task-list endpoint p99 latency 1.8-2.4s during US East business hours
Send written acknowledgement of known latency issue to Globex Corp

emails.subject: Cost Optimisation Audit — $8,400/mo overspend identified, needs team input
emails.body: Hey Alice, finished the AWS cost audit for Q2. We are spending $8,400/month more than budgeted. Main culprits: (1) 3 m5.2xlarge EC2 instances running at < 5% CPU — these were spun up for the load test in May and never terminated. That's $2,100/month of waste. Terminating Monday unless you tell me otherwise. (2) CloudWatch log retention set to indefinite on all services — costs $3,200/month. Recommend 90-day retention policy. Need your approval to change this as it affects compliance logs. (3) The staging environment is running 24/7 — $3,100/month. Can we put it on a schedule (e.g. off 22:00–06:00 UTC)? Need a decision from you on (2) and (3) before end of week.
hidden_action_items: Approve CloudWatch log retention policy change to 90 days (impacts compliance) by end of week
Decide on staging environment schedule (off 22:00-06:00 UTC) to save $3,100/month

emails.subject: GDPR Compliance Audit — Engineering must provide DPA evidence by June 30
emails.body: Alice, our annual GDPR compliance audit starts July 7. The auditors require evidence from engineering by June 30: (1) Data Processing Agreement with Groq (our LLM provider) — I need confirmation that DPA is signed or that we have written confirmation they are GDPR-compliant. (2) A record of all systems that process EU personal data — please provide an up-to-date list. (3) Evidence that PII scrubbing is applied before data reaches any third-party service. Engineering needs to own items 1 and 3. If we cannot provide evidence, we may need to pause LLM features for EU customers until audit is complete. Please respond by June 28.
hidden_action_items: Obtain signed DPA from Groq or written GDPR compliance confirmation by June 28
Provide list of all systems processing EU personal data to legal team by June 28
Provide evidence of PII scrubbing applied before third-party LLM calls by June 28

emails.subject: Action Required: Stripe API version migration — v2023-10-16 deprecated Sep 1
emails.body: Hi Alice, this is a reminder that the Stripe API version your account uses (2023-10-16) will be deprecated on September 1, 2026. After that date, requests using this version will return errors. You should migrate to the latest stable version (2026-03-01) before the deprecation date. The migration guide is at stripe.com/docs/upgrades. The main breaking changes relevant to your usage are in the Payment Intent and Subscription objects. We recommend completing migration by August 1 to allow time for testing. Please confirm migration is in your roadmap — reply to this email or update your developer dashboard.
hidden_action_items: Migrate Stripe API version from 2023-10-16 to 2026-03-01 before September 1
Confirm Stripe API migration is in Q3 roadmap — reply to Stripe or update dashboard

emails.subject: PR #342 ready for review — pgBouncer connection pooling
emails.body: Alice, PR #342 implementing pgBouncer is ready for review. I've tested it on staging and connection pool exhaustion no longer happens at 500 concurrent users — p95 latency dropped from 1,800 ms to 340 ms in load tests. There's one thing I want your input on: I had to change the database URL format in the connection string which may need a coordinated config update across all services simultaneously. There's a risk window of about 2 minutes during the switchover. I've documented the rollout plan in the PR description. Could you review by Thursday? We'd like to deploy Friday morning before US East wakes up. Also looping you in — I've not been able to reproduce the memory leak locally, might need production heap snapshot access.
hidden_action_items: Review PR #342 (pgBouncer) by Thursday — coordinated deploy Friday morning
Approve production heap snapshot access for Marco to debug WebSocket memory leak

emails.subject: Interview panel needed — Senior Backend Engineer — interviews start June 24
emails.body: Hi Alice, we have 4 candidates for the Senior Backend Engineer role shortlisted and ready for technical interviews. Interviews are scheduled for June 24-25. We need you to nominate 3 engineers for the technical panel (each interview is 1 hour). Please also confirm whether you want a system design round or a take-home assignment as the second stage — the team is split. HR needs the panel names by Monday June 23 to send calendar invites. Also, the job description for the Staff Engineer role (your team's req) needs updating before we post it — current JD is from 2024. Can you review and update by end of week?
hidden_action_items: Nominate 3 engineers for Senior Backend interview panel by Monday June 23
Decide: system design round or take-home for second stage — reply to HR
Update Staff Engineer job description before end of week
